item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Markets are in a recovery phase with equities extended above long-term averages and volatility subdued, but elevated oil volatility and rising yields pose conditional risks to cyclical and growth names. Monitor yields and OVX for signs of stress; otherwise current conditions appear stable.
Market summary: - Regime: Recovery (🟢) - SPY: 733.83, trading above 200-day MA (672.40) — upward trend, conditions stable - QQQ: 695.77, above 100-day MA (616.73) — tech breadth supportive - ARKK: three-month change +19.98% — strong momentum in innovation names - VIX: 17.39 — low/moderate volatility environment - TNX (10Y yield): 4.36% — higher yield environment that can pressure long-duration assets - Income spread (S&P dividend yield 1.01% vs 10Y 4.36%): spread = -3.35% — favors bonds over equities - OVX: 72.34 (regime: mid) — oil volatility elevated, a risk for energy-sensitive sectors - Mortgage rate: 6.37% (Condition: Neutral) — housing financing remains a headwind but stable - Overall risk posture: equity-friendly momentum but watch rising yields and elevated OVX; raw data available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Market regime is Recovery with risk tilted toward continued equity upside, but elevated commodity volatility and real yields create cross-currents. Equity momentum is constructive while volatility and higher bond yields pose headline risk—position sizing and duration management remain important.
Market summary: - Regime: Recovery (stable conditions) - SPY: 723.77, well above 200-day MA 671.87 — bullish trend - QQQ: 681.61 vs 100-day MA 616.05 — strong tech leadership - VIX: 17.38 — moderate market calm, stable - TNX / 10Y yield: 4.43% — higher-rate environment vs recent years, a constraint on duration - Income spread (S&P dividend yield 1.01% vs 10Y 4.43%): spread = -3.42 — favors bonds (Bond Yield Advantage) - ARKK: three-month change +9.42% — risk-on flows into growth/innovation - OVX: 74.73 (regime: mid) — elevated oil volatility that could drive equity dispersion - Mortgage rate: 6.3% (condition: Neutral) — housing cost remains a headwind but stable - Overall: equity trend and momentum supportive, but higher yields and commodity volatility argue for selective exposure and active risk controls
Raw data available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Equity markets are in recovery with bullish trend signals, but volatility and elevated commodity/Oil volatility plus higher bond yields create risk for rotation and rate-sensitive sectors. Income looks relatively more attractive in bonds given the negative spread versus the S&P dividend; monitor VIX and TNX for regime shifts—conditions currently stable but vigilantly watch yield moves and OVX.
Market summary: - Market regime: Recovery (🟢) - SPY price: 718.01 — trading above 200-day MA (ma200 671.39) ⇒ bullish/SPY trend: positive - QQQ price: 672.88 — above 100-day MA (ma100 615.48) ⇒ tech trend: positive - VIX: 18.29 — moderate/low volatility - TNX (10Y yield): 4.45% — higher yield environment; yield context: supportive of income assets, pressure for rate-sensitive equities - Income spread (SP dividend yield 1.02% vs 10Y 4.45%): spread = -3.43% — favors bonds (Bond Yield Advantage) - OVX: 78.63 — mid regime (elevated oil volatility) - ARKK: three-month change +6.52% — active growth/innovation momentum - Mortgage rate: 6.3% — condition: Neutral - Overall risk stance: stable for now; higher yields and commodity volatility are key risks to monitor
Raw data available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Equity markets are in a recovery phase, supported by trending prices and moderate volatility, but elevated oil volatility and rising yields pose downside risks to growth-sensitive sectors. Monitor bond yields and volatility for signs the recovery could be derailed; if yields continue to climb, defensives and yield-bearing assets may outperform.
Market summary: - Regime: Recovery (stable) - SPY: 720.65, trading above its 200-day MA (ma200 670.94) — uptrend intact - QQQ: 674.15, above its 100-day MA (ma100 615.0) — momentum supportive - VIX: 16.99 — moderate volatility, conditions stable - TNX / 10Y yield: 4.39% — higher-yield environment, watch for tightening financial conditions - Income spread (S&P dividend yield vs 10Y): SP dividend yield 1.03% vs 10Y 4.39% → spread -3.36% (Bond Yield Advantage) — income spread favors bonds - OVX (oil volatility): 75.4, regime mid — elevated energy volatility is a risk to cyclicals - ARKK: three-month change +3.44% — modest recovery in growth/innovation exposure - Mortgage rate: Unknown; condition: Unknown (explicitly unknown in snapshot) - Raw data available at /data
If conditions remain unchanged these readings are stable; watch yields and OVX for early signs of regime shift.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Risk-on tone in this recovery phase is supported by equity strength and moderate volatility, but elevated oil volatility and still-high nominal yields keep drawdown risk and sector rotation active. Position sizing should account for interest-rate sensitivity and potential commodity-driven inflation shocks.
Market summary: - Regime: Recovery (stable) - SPY: 720.65 vs 200-day MA 670.94 — trend bullish, above long-term MA - QQQ: 674.15 vs 100-day MA 615.0 — leadership intact, momentum positive - VIX: 16.99 — moderate/contained equity volatility - TNX (10Y): 4.40% — yields elevated; interest-rate backdrop favors bond sensitivity - Income spread: S&P dividend yield 1.02% vs 10Y 4.40% → spread = -3.38% (income spread favors bonds) - Mortgage rate: 6.3% — condition: Neutral - OVX: 75.4 (regime: mid) — oil volatility elevated, watch energy/commodity risk - ARKK 3-month change: +3.44% — selective growth recovery - Other: raw data available in /data
(Conditions described above are stable unless noted otherwise.)
